title: Berry Curvature Closedness Repeats The Same Stokes Sum
summary: Away from the singular points, the Berry curvature is closed; applying Stokes's theorem on the punctured Brillouin zone gives the same zero-sum law as the degree-theory proof.
assumptions: The Berry connection is defined away from the bad points.
The node set is isolated so the punctured Brillouin zone is smooth.
regime: Berry-geometric proof on the punctured Brillouin zone.
scope: Lecture One proof fragment that recovers Nielsen-Ninomiya from Berry curvature.

source_anchors.notes: Witten rederives the same global sum rule from dF=0 away from the bad points.

representation: Berry-curvature proof fragment paralleling the degree-theory Stokes argument.

math_expressions.notes: Berry curvature is closed away from the singular locus.
The Berry-curvature boundary sum reproduces charge cancellation.

step_justification: Closedness of F away from bad points makes the punctured-zone bulk integral vanish.
The boundary spheres around the bad points capture the Berry-flux charges.
Summing those boundary integrals gives the zero-total-charge statement.
retrieval_hints: Use when a derivation answer must give the Berry-curvature route to Nielsen-Ninomiya.
